text: I'm such a mongrel. I'm from India originally, but have moved just a bunch of countries from the age of 12, so a little bit from everywhere.
context: V describing his multicultural background when asked about origins - frames identity through geographic journey rather than single origin.

text: You have to be in a semi at a moment's notice. I have to be on and going state.
context: Describing the founder mental state during holidays when customers still need attention - captures the always-ready mode of startup life.

text: You develop that intuition for who you're going to enjoy, like, spending time with after enough of these.
context: Discussing the social scene dynamics and how experienced networkers learn to identify high-value connections quickly.

text: There's a lot to career coaching that leads them to making terrible fucking decisions using auto apply tools - some real dog shit tools out there that are just sucking people's money up.
context: V critiquing the predatory job search tool ecosystem that exploits desperate job seekers.

text: We like to think of skills as like clusters of co-occurring things - comorbidities is the wrong word, but you know, like the co-occurring things.
context: Explaining Careerspan's skills ontology - uses medical metaphor (comorbidities) then course-corrects to a more neutral framing.

text: Holy shit, we've built this like really high signal community of folks. And unlike every other platform, we have deep data on them.
context: V's realization moment about Careerspan's unique asset - combining community quality with unprecedented data depth.

text: It's a crowded marketplace, it's very hard to distinguish ourselves and we get a lot of loyalty, but we want to make some money.
context: Candid acknowledgment of the B2C job search market challenges that drove the pivot to B2B.

text: It's kind of like if I asked you to describe what's your relationship with the color blue - it's not false, it's just like fuzzy in a way that makes us nervous.
context: Explaining why traditional skills-matching feels unreliable compared to Careerspan's structured elicitation approach.

text: We want folks that are 'Goldilocks passive' - that's how I think about it. If they get an email about an opportunity that's attractive enough, they might like spring to action.
context: Defining the ideal candidate persona for the platform - neither desperate nor completely unavailable.

text: We are pushing a message to them that says, 'Hey, there's a cool opportunity that you are probably a great fit for. We've already vouched for you. All you gotta do is like chat.'
context: Describing the low-friction candidate experience where Careerspan pre-qualifies and reduces barriers to engagement.
